Section 88001(b) of the Government Code provides, in
applicable part:
The bal1ot pamphlet shall contain: (b) A copy of
the specific constitutional or statutory
provision, if any, which would be repealed or
revised by each state measure.
Section 83002(e) of the Government Cocle provicles, in
applicable part:
The baIlot pamphlet shaII contain as to each state
measure to be voted upon, . . . (e) . . . the existing
provisions of law repealed or revisecl by the
mea su re .
It is my understanding that the Legislative Counsel
believes that the provisions of the Government Code require
him to provide, pursuant to Government Code section
88005.5, "strike-out text" with respect to the sections
specifica]ly referenced in section six of Proposition 119.
"Strike-out text" of these sections was inclucled in the
1984 General Election Ba1lot Pamphlet with respect to
Proposition 39. (See enclosed copy of a page from that
pamphlet. )
The impact of including the "strike-out text" with respect
to Proposition l19 is substantial. 
We estimate that
including this material wiII adcl an additional twenty-five
pages to the pamphlet at an additional cost for DrintjJlg
and postaqe of S937,500. The additional pages will make
the pamphlet itself more intimidating to the voters and
therefore less useful and it may also delay receipt of the
pamphlet because of the ailditional printing time required.
(The fact that four additional measures are expected to be
placed on the June Primary baIlot by the Legislature nearly
a month after the current statutory cleadline for doing so
has become a serious logistical matter in itself since the
completed pamphlet is scheduled to go on public display on
Friday, February 16, I990 ! )
I see no benefit for including this material in every
pamphLet. As can be seen from the example included from
Proposition 39, the information is useless to all but a
select few voters in this state.
Given the fact that the Ianguage of section six of
Proposition t19 itself defers repeal of the referenced
sections of the Elections Code, that including the
additional pages of "strike-out text" will have significant
negative impact, and that including the material witl not
provide any useful information to voters not otherwise
L


3
available, I propose to publish the "strike-out text" in a
supplement availabte on request rather than including it in
the ballot pamphlet itself. 
Please advise whether this
approach is consistent with my obligations under the
Political Reform Act .
Unfortunately, I must have an immediate answer. The text
of the pamphlet is scheduled for public display pursuant to
Government Code section 88006 on February f6, 1990. The
printing itself must begin on March 12, L990. Your
immediate attention to this matter wou1d, therefore, be
appreciated.
My chief deputy, Tony Miller, (5-637f) is available to
provide additional information to your staff.
Sincerely,

Donalil E. MaIe, State Printer

sections.question: Please advise whether this approach is consistent with my obligations under the Political Reform Act. I propose to publish the "strike-out text" in a supplement available on request rather than including it in the ballot pamphlet itself.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether California's Secretary of State can publish required "strike-out text" for Proposition 119 in a supplement available on request rather than including it in the ballot pamphlet itself, given the substantial cost ($937,500) and logistical burden of including 25 additional pages.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from Secretary of State to FPPC Chair John Larson, not an FPPC response. Document quality is poor with scanning artifacts affecting readability. Request seeks expedited advice given February 16, 1990 deadline. No FPPC conclusion or advice letter content present.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Please advise whether this approach is consistent with my obligations under the Political Reform Act. I propose to publish the "strike-out text" in a supplement available on request rather than including it in the ballot pamphlet itself.

embedding.summary: The California Secretary of State (March Fong Eu) requests FPPC advice on whether she can publish "strike-out text" required for Proposition 119 in a supplement rather than in the ballot pamphlet itself, due to significant printing costs and logistical concerns.
